summary: Removed the now-empty assembly stub asm/melee/pl/player.s and dropped its object from obj_files.mk because the player file was matched in C and no longer needed an empty assembly section placeholder.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/pl/player.s, which only contained macros.inc plus empty .text, .data, .bss, .sdata, and .sdata2 section declarations.
Kept $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/pl/player.o in the object list, indicating the source-side player object was the active matched implementation.
smart_moves: Cleaned up the build list at the same time as deleting the unused assembly file, avoiding a stale object reference.
Removed an empty assembly placeholder only after the corresponding file was described as matched, reducing redundant asm while preserving the src/melee/pl/player.o build target.
Used a minimal deletion-only PR for a mechanically safe cleanup with low review burden.
decomp_lessons: When a C translation unit fully replaces an asm placeholder, delete the empty .s file and remove its asm object from obj_files.mk rather than leaving empty sections in the tree.
Check for paired src and asm object entries in TEXT_O_FILES; once the src object covers the file, the asm object may become unnecessary.
Empty section-only assembly files can persist after matching work and should be cleaned up to keep the object list meaningful.
naming_conventions: Player module source object used path pattern $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/pl/player.o.
Assembly fallback/stub object used path pattern $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/pl/player.o.
Neighboring unmatched assembly object retained the address-style name code_8003715C.o.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace asm stubs with matched C objects by removing the asm file from TEXT_O_FILES once the source object is present.
Do not keep empty .text/.data/.bss/.sdata/.sdata2 section declarations as a placeholder after a file has matched.
This PR provides evidence of a cleanup tactic, not a new matching technique; no function-level matching details were included.
follow_up_queries: Find other empty asm files containing only section declarations that still appear in obj_files.mk.
Search for modules where both src/.../*.o and asm/.../*.o are listed for the same logical file.
Review subsequent player module PRs for additional cleanup around asm/melee/pl/code_8003715C.o.
